$schema_note: Sprint brief for the Sanders Intelligence app. One file per phase. Each task is a self-contained implementation prompt: outline, technical requirements, expected results, test coverage. Generated by the sprint-planner skill.
cadence: 2-day phase (Day 1: small wins + verifications; Day 2: redesign + broad QA)
theme: Fresh-ask cleanup (task workflow polish + 'Today' redesign) + MVP polish (Inbound verify + broad QA pass)

project.name: Sanders intelligence app
project.stack: React 18 + TypeScript + Vite, Tailwind, React Router v6, TanStack Query v5, Supabase, Recharts

selection_rationale: All 5 picks from sprint 2026-05-30 shipped and are deployed on origin/main as of commit e51bba50. User picked 'fresh asks' + 'MVP polish' for this phase, with explicit 'promote UX nits' direction. The fresh asks cluster around the task page (3 user-reported polish items added 2026-05-29) plus a 'My Day' redesign (also 2026-05-29) - all are direct user feedback after using the just-shipped task workflow. The MVP picks (Inbound verify, broad QA pass) close out two of the three MVP polish items that have been open since 2026-05-05; the third (Mobile responsiveness) is the heaviest and is deferred to the next phase so it benefits from these polish fixes landing first. News feed and Mobile responsiveness pushed to the following phase.
instructions_for_implementer: Work tasks in the 'order' field. For EACH task: read 'clarifying_questions' first and ask the user before writing code. Treat 'likely_files' as starting points for investigation, not the full surface. Implement against 'requirements', validate against 'completion_criteria', and write/extend tests per 'test_coverage'. When done, update the matching Asana task's completion status and leave a comment citing the file/line evidence (mirror the convention used 2026-06-04 when closing the prior batch).

1215004196463583 - Phase 2 DoD view (pushed to 2026-06-12; scope likely shrunk by the 'Came back' section that just shipped)
sprint_summary.test_coverage_backlog_status: Five test-coverage tickets remain open (exportCsv 2026-06-05, useTaskComments 2026-06-08, InboundPipeline helpers 2026-06-08, hook tests 2026-06-11, auth flows 2026-06-15). Treated as background work; pick one up alongside any sprint task that touches the same area.

tasks.title: UX: Daily View subtitle apostrophe fix
tasks.outline: DailyView.tsx subtitle reads 'Andrew work for May 28, 2026'. Add the missing apostrophe so it reads 'Andrew's work for ...'. Five-character change. Tag onto the first DailyView touch in this phase (task #5).
tasks.technical_description: app/src/pages/work/DailyView.tsx line 93 currently renders `{profile?.name ?? 'Your'} work for {fmtDate(...)}`. Update to render `{profile?.name ?? 'Your'}'s work for {fmtDate(...)}`. Handle the trailing-s edge case ('Chris's work' vs 'Chris' work') per AP style - modern usage allows 's even for names ending in s, so the simple template above is fine.
likely_files: app/src/pages/work/DailyView.tsx - line 93 specifically
requirements: Subtitle includes the possessive apostrophe.
Render unchanged when profile.name is null (fallback 'Your' becomes "Your's" which is grammatically wrong; switch fallback to 'Today's' in that case).
completion_criteria: Subtitle reads 'Andrew's work for June 5, 2026' (or equivalent for any user).
When unauthenticated/no profile, subtitle reads 'Today's work for ...'.
No console errors, no TypeScript errors, existing DailyView tests still pass.
expected_outcomes: Small but visible polish on the page every user sees first.

unit: Optional: snapshot test in DailyView.static.test.ts asserting the subtitle text format.
manual_qa: Load /daily as Andrew - confirm 'Andrew's work for ...'.
Sign out and visit /daily - confirm fallback wording (or redirect).

clarifying_questions: Use 's even for names ending in s, or apply AP-style apostrophe-only? Recommendation: use 's (modern, simpler, no special-case).
tasks.pre_implementation_instruction: If you're already editing DailyView.tsx for task #5 ('Today' redesign), bundle this fix into the same commit to avoid an extra round-trip.

tasks.title: BUG: Remove non-functional trash icon from task list rows
tasks.outline: Per user report (2026-05-29): the trash icon on task list rows doesn't do anything, and there's already a Cancel action that serves the 'remove this task' purpose. Pull the icon out so the row UI stops promising an action it doesn't deliver.
tasks.technical_description: app/src/components/tasks/TaskCard.tsx renders a Trash2 icon button (visible in the QA screenshots from 2026-05-28). If the click handler is wired to useDeleteTask, verify whether it's actually firing - if it's a no-op then the underlying mutation may also be broken. Either way, the right answer per user request is to remove the icon entirely; Cancel + Postpone cover the lifecycle, and hard-delete is an admin-only operation that lives in the Edit modal or a dedicated admin route.
likely_files: app/src/components/tasks/TaskCard.tsx - remove the Trash2 button and its onDelete prop
app/src/pages/tasks/TasksPage.tsx - drop the onDelete prop pass-through and unused useDeleteTask import if it becomes unused
app/src/pages/work/DailyView.tsx - same prop cleanup if it also passed onDelete to TaskCard
app/src/pages/tasks/TasksTable.tsx - check if the table renders a delete affordance; if yes, remove or relocate
requirements: Trash icon no longer renders on TaskCard or TasksTable rows.
onDelete prop removed from TaskCard interface; callers updated.
useDeleteTask hook itself stays in useTasks.ts (may be needed by an admin tool later); just stop calling it from row UI.
Cancel action remains and works as before.
completion_criteria: No trash icon on /tasks, /daily, or anywhere TaskCard renders.
Cancel + Postpone flows unaffected.
TypeScript compiles with no unused-import warnings.
Existing TaskCard/TasksPage tests still pass after the prop removal.
expected_outcomes: Row UI stops promising an action it doesn't deliver. Reduces user confusion and matches the documented task lifecycle (todo -> in_progress -> done | cancelled | postponed).

unit: Extend TaskCard.static.test.ts (if it exists) or TasksPage.helpers.test.ts: assert TaskCard render output contains no element with title='Delete task' or role='button' with the Trash2 icon class.
integration: TasksPage.helpers.test.ts: render TasksPage with a 1-row fixture, query for delete-related selectors, assert none present.
manual_qa: Visit /tasks - confirm no trash icon on any row.
Open Cancel modal - confirms it still works.

clarifying_questions: Should hard-delete remain available anywhere (e.g., admin-only context menu in the Edit modal) or be fully removed from the UI? Recommendation: remove from row UI only; if admins need it, expose later as a deliberate Edit-modal action with a confirmation.

Should we add a brief inline banner ('Use Cancel to remove tasks') the first time a user looks for the missing icon, or skip the hand-holding? Recommendation: skip - the Cancel action is right there next to where the trash used to be.
tasks.pre_implementation_instruction: Ask the clarifying questions and wait for explicit direction before editing files.

tasks.title: Feature: Make notes mandatory when cancelling or postponing a task
tasks.outline: Per user report (2026-05-29): the TaskActionNoteModal currently allows empty submission when cancelling or postponing. Force a non-empty note so the audit trail (task_comments with kind='cancel'/'postpone') is always populated. Improves accountability and reduces 'why did we close this?' confusion.
tasks.technical_description: app/src/components/tasks/TaskActionNoteModal.tsx renders the note textarea + Save button. Currently the Save button submits regardless of note content. Add validation: Save button disabled when the trimmed note is empty; inline error if the user tries to submit empty (defensive). The handleActionSubmit callers in DailyView.tsx and TasksPage.tsx already conditionally write the comment only when note.trim() is non-empty - that branch becomes always-true after this change, which is fine.
likely_files: app/src/components/tasks/TaskActionNoteModal.tsx - add disabled state on Save + minimum-length validation
app/src/__tests__/TaskActionNoteModal.test.tsx - NEW or extension: assert disabled Save when empty, enabled when non-empty
app/src/pages/work/DailyView.tsx + app/src/pages/tasks/TasksPage.tsx - the handleActionSubmit callers: assert note.trim() is non-empty (defense in depth; UI should already prevent this)
requirements: TaskActionNoteModal Save button is disabled when textarea is empty OR whitespace-only.
Save button is enabled the moment the textarea contains at least one non-whitespace character.
Optional: visible helper text under the textarea ('Note required') when the field is empty.
Keyboard submit (Enter on Save / Cmd+Enter in textarea) respects the same validation - no submit on empty.
Existing manually-cancelled tasks that have no note: unchanged (this only affects new cancellations / postponements going forward).
completion_criteria: Cancel or postpone a task; the Save button is disabled until a note is entered.
Cannot bypass via Enter/Cmd+Enter.
After saving, the resulting task_comments row has kind='cancel' (or 'postpone') and a non-empty body.
Existing DailyView and TasksPage tests still pass; new tests cover the disabled state.
No TypeScript errors.
expected_outcomes: Every cancelled or postponed task now has an audit-trail comment. Closes a small but real accountability hole.

unit: TaskActionNoteModal.test.tsx: empty textarea -> Save disabled; type 'x' -> Save enabled; clear -> Save disabled.
Trimming: textarea value '   ' (whitespace only) -> Save disabled.
integration: RTL render the modal, attempt to click disabled Save, assert onSubmit was not called.
manual_qa: Click Cancel on a task; confirm Save is disabled until typing; submit; reload page; confirm the comment shows in Timeline.

clarifying_questions: Minimum note length: 1 character or 5 characters? Recommendation: 1 (the requirement is 'force a note', not 'force a useful note' - the latter is a policy issue, not a UI one).

Should we enforce mandatory notes on Done as well (currently optional via the completion prompt that 1215179791172798 added)? Recommendation: NO - cancellation/postponement need a reason; completion can be implicit ('I did it').
tasks.pre_implementation_instruction: Ask the clarifying questions and wait for explicit direction before editing files.

tasks.title: MVP: Inbound Pipeline - verify arrival-month grouping and ETA accuracy
tasks.outline: MVP checklist item open since 2026-05-05. Verify the Units-by-Estimated-Arrival-Month chart on /purchasing/inbound bins SKUs correctly: a SKU's estimated arrival = (upload_date + lead_time_days) and should fall into the bin for the calendar month containing that date. Also verify the table's Est. Arrival column matches.
tasks.technical_description:
Inbound Pipeline lives at app/src/pages/purchasing/InboundPipeline.tsx (~400 lines). The arrival-month derivation is currently inline (no helpers extracted - tracked separately as 1215143557584651). For this verification:
(1) Pick 10 SKUs from the table with diverse lead_time_days (0, 30, 60, 90, 120+).
(2) Compute expected arrival month manually: upload.uploaded_at + lead_time_days days, take the YYYY-MM.
(3) Confirm the chart bar for that month includes those SKUs' on_order units.
(4) Confirm the Est. Arrival column in the table reads the same month.
(5) Spot-check edge cases: lead_time=0 (should arrive this month), null lead_time (should fall into 'Unknown' bin or be excluded - confirm behavior).
likely_files: app/src/pages/purchasing/InboundPipeline.tsx - the chart data computation + table rendering
app/src/lib/utils.ts - estimatedArrivalMonth and parseMonthLabel helpers if they exist there
requirements: Verification run produces a written report (in the Asana task closing comment): which SKUs were checked, expected vs actual bin, pass/fail per row.
If discrepancies found: file a new BUG ticket linked from this one with repro details. Do NOT silently fix - that's a separate scope of work.
If everything matches: close with a note 'Verified on 2026-06-05 against upload (18).csv, 10 SKU sample'.
completion_criteria: 10-SKU sample verified; results documented in the Asana task closing comment.
Any discrepancies filed as separate BUG tickets with file:line repro.
Edge cases (lead_time=0, null lead_time, lead_time > 365 days) explicitly noted in the report.
expected_outcomes: MVP item closed with confidence. Either the chart is correct (great, document it) or we have actionable bug tickets with repro steps.

unit: If a helper like estimatedArrivalMonth(upload_date, lead_time_days) exists, extend utils.test.ts: cover lead_time 0 / 30 / 90 / null / negative.
If not extracted yet, do NOT do that here - it's tracked separately as 1215143557584651; just note it in the closing comment.
manual_qa: Sort the table by Est. Arrival - confirm the order matches the chart x-axis.
Toggle the 'All arrivals' filter to 'Near (0-30d)' / 'Mid (31-90d)' / 'Long (90+d)' - confirm the filter respects the same date math as the chart.

clarifying_questions: Sample size - 10 SKUs OK, or should we be more rigorous (50+)? Recommendation: 10 is enough for a spot-check; if any fail, expand the sample.

Should we also verify against the original CSV (compare lead_time_days in CSV vs the value rendered) or trust the upload pipeline? Recommendation: trust the pipeline; that's its own ticket if we need to verify.
tasks.pre_implementation_instruction: This is a verification task, not an implementation task. No code changes expected unless a bug is discovered (in which case file a separate ticket).

tasks.title: Rename 'My Day' to 'Today'; show all due tasks with a 'Your tasks' sub-section + global created-today counter
tasks.outline: Per user feedback (2026-05-29): the 'My Day' page is too narrow - it only shows the logged-in user's tasks. The user wants 'Today' which shows ALL tasks due today (so a purchasing lead can see the team's load) with a clear 'Your tasks' sub-section pinned at the top, and a header counter that shows total newly-created tasks across the whole team (not just this user).
tasks.technical_description:
app/src/pages/work/DailyView.tsx currently filters everything to profile.id via partitionDailyTasks(tasks, profile.id) (line 34) and computeDailyCounters(tasks, profile.id). Update:
(1) Rename the route from /daily to /today (or alias both with a redirect from /daily for back-compat).
(2) Update sidebar label 'My Day' -> 'Today'.
(3) Page header: 'Today - June 5, 2026'.
(4) New section layout:
   - Counter row (unchanged structure): 'Created today (team)', 'Completed today (team)', 'Due today (you)'.
   - 'Your tasks (N)' section - assigned_to=me, due_date=today or status=in_progress (current 'Today' bucket logic).
   - 'All other tasks due today (N)' section - assigned_to!=me OR null, due_date=today.
   - 'Came back' section (existing).
   - 'Completed yesterday (you)' section (existing, unchanged).
(5) The 'All other tasks' rows render with assignee chip prominently so the user can see who owns each.
(6) Tasks in 'All other tasks' are read-only on this page (no Advance/Cancel buttons) to prevent stepping on toes; clicking opens the existing TaskModal.
likely_files: app/src/App.tsx - new /today route, redirect from /daily
app/src/components/layout/Sidebar.tsx - rename label
app/src/pages/work/DailyView.tsx -> rename to TodayView.tsx (preserve git history with `git mv`), restructure sections
app/src/pages/work/DailyView.helpers.ts -> rename to TodayView.helpers.ts; add partitionTodayTasks(tasks, profileId) returning { yourTasks, otherTasks, completedYesterday, cameBack, counters }
app/src/__tests__/DailyView.helpers.test.ts -> rename + extend with new partitions
CLAUDE.md - update Route Map: /daily becomes /today (note redirect)
Apostrophe fix from task #1 lands in this file naturally.
requirements: Route /today serves the new page; /daily redirects to /today (avoid breaking bookmarks).
Sidebar label updated.
Page shows: counter row (Created today team, Completed today team, Due today YOU), Your tasks section, All other tasks due today section, Came back section, Completed yesterday section.
'Your tasks' and 'Other tasks' sections both show empty states.
All-other-tasks rows are read-only at the row level (no Advance/Cancel/Postpone buttons) but still clickable to open TaskModal.
Counter row 'Created today (team)' counts ALL tasks where created_at::date = today regardless of assignee.
Counter row 'Completed today (team)' similarly team-wide.
'Due today (you)' stays user-scoped.
Apostrophe fix from task #1 lands here.
Subtitle reads 'Today - <date>' rather than '<name>'s work for <date>' (the subtitle from task #1 is now misleading given the broader scope).
completion_criteria: Navigate to /today as admin Andrew: see Your tasks (Andrew's), Other tasks (everyone else's due today), Came back, Completed yesterday.
Navigate to /daily: redirects to /today.
Sidebar 'Today' link is highlighted on /today.
Counter 'Created today (team)' matches the count of all tasks with created_at = today across all users.
Click a row in 'Other tasks' - opens TaskModal pre-populated, no Advance button visible on the card itself.
No console errors, no TypeScript errors, all existing tests still pass (after the helpers rename).
expected_outcomes: Purchasing lead can see the entire team's workload in one place, not just their own queue. Closes the 'why is My Day empty when I see 27 tasks elsewhere' confusion from QA on 2026-05-28.

unit: - partitionTodayTasks correctly bins yourTasks (assignee=me, due=today OR in_progress), otherTasks (assignee!=me, due=today), cameBack, completedYesterday for a 10-row fixture.
- counters: createdToday and completedToday count team-wide regardless of assignee; dueToday counts only assignee=me.
- Edge: task with assignee=null and due=today goes into otherTasks bucket.
- Edge: task with assignee=me and status=cancelled is NOT in yourTasks (only todo + in_progress).
integration: RTL render TodayView with a fixture; assert section headers + row counts; click an other-tasks row and assert TaskModal opens (mocked).
Empty states: render with no tasks; assert each section's empty message.
manual_qa: Sign in as Andrew; visit /today; confirm Your tasks shows Andrew's queue (probably empty), Other tasks shows everyone else's due-today.
Sign in as Mordechai; same check from his perspective - his tasks should now appear in Your tasks.
Apostrophe check: subtitle reads 'Today - June 5, 2026' (no user name needed in the broader view).

clarifying_questions: Route name confirmation: /today (recommended) or keep /daily as the canonical and add 'Today' as a label?

Should 'All other tasks' be filtered to the user's department (purchasing user sees only purchasing tasks) or workspace-wide? Recommendation: department-scoped to match the existing useTasks() role gate.

Should there be a 4th sub-section 'Unassigned (N) - claim' on /today, replacing the 1215210678717164 ticket entirely? Recommendation: YES if scope allows - puts the unassigned-claim feature in its natural home and closes that ticket as part of this work.
Subtitle: 'Today - June 5, 2026' or include user name on the right side ('Andrew')? Recommendation: 'Today - June 5, 2026' centered; user name lives in sidebar footer already.
tasks.pre_implementation_instruction: Ask the clarifying questions and wait for explicit direction before editing files. The 'absorb 1215210678717164' decision affects whether we close that ticket as part of this work.

tasks.title: MVP: QA pass - error states, empty states, loading states across all views
tasks.outline: MVP checklist item open since 2026-05-05. Systematic walk of all 10 routes (now including /today) to verify three states render correctly per view: (a) loading state during initial data fetch, (b) error state when the data fetch fails, (c) empty state when there are zero records. File a ticket for any view that fails any of the three.
tasks.technical_description:
For each route, simulate three scenarios:
(1) Loading: hard-reload with throttled connection (Chrome DevTools 'Slow 3G') - confirm a loading state renders (not a blank black screen, not a stuck spinner).
(2) Error: temporarily revoke Supabase RLS to force a query failure, OR mock-fail in dev tools - confirm an error state renders with a helpful message and retry affordance.
(3) Empty: log in as a user whose role-filter returns zero rows, OR query a fresh upload with no data - confirm an empty state renders with appropriate copy.

Routes to walk: /login, /reset-password, /, /purchasing/action-center, /purchasing/inventory, /purchasing/inbound, /purchasing/vendors, /executive, /executive/departments, /today, /tasks, /admin/users, /admin/uploads.
likely_files: All page-level components in app/src/pages/**/*.tsx
app/src/components/ui/LoadingSpinner.tsx + PageLoader - reuse for loading state consistency
app/src/components/ui/ErrorBoundary.tsx - catches render errors; check if it's wrapping each route
If a view fails: file a focused BUG ticket per page with screenshot + repro
requirements: Per-route checklist filled out and saved as the closing comment on this Asana task.
Any failing state filed as a separate BUG ticket (do NOT silently fix here - that's separate scope).
Use the qa-runner skill's checklist as the starting template; extend with the three-states columns.
Cover all 13 routes listed above.
Include /today (added in task #5) - critical to verify the new redesign's three states.
completion_criteria: 13-route checklist completed and pasted into the Asana closing comment.
Each failing state has its own BUG ticket filed with screenshot evidence and repro steps.
Asana task closed with a summary: 'Verified N/13 views; M issues filed' with links to the new tickets.
expected_outcomes: Pre-launch confidence. Either every view handles all three states gracefully (great, MVP item closed) or we have a prioritized list of fixes.

unit: If patterns emerge (e.g., 'every page should wrap in <PageLoader>'), consider extracting a hook usePageLoadStates() and unit-testing it. Optional this phase.
integration: Bonus: add a smoke test per page that renders with isLoading=true / error=Error('x') / data=[] and asserts the right state appears. Defer to next phase if scope is tight.
manual_qa: Walk all 13 routes through all 3 states. ~3-4 hours total at a steady pace.
Use Chrome DevTools throttling (Slow 3G) for loading; mock-revoke RLS or use a test user with no role grants for error; use a freshly-deactivated user for empty.

clarifying_questions: Should I file bugs for cosmetic issues (e.g., 'spinner is centered awkwardly') or only functional ones (e.g., 'page never recovers from error')? Recommendation: functional only; track cosmetic in a 'UX nits' rollup ticket.
How granular should empty-state copy be? Per-page custom messages or a shared 'No data yet' component? Recommendation: per-page custom; the shared component is a good follow-up but out of scope here.

Should the QA pass include the QA-runner skill auto-running, or is this a manual walk? Recommendation: manual + qa-runner together - run the skill to capture the smoke baseline, then dive into the three-states checks manually.
tasks.pre_implementation_instruction: This is a verification task. The deliverable is a per-route checklist + BUG tickets for any failures. No proactive code changes. Use the qa-runner skill at D:\Sanders Intelligence\skills\qa-runner\ as the starting template.

sprint_close_checklist: All 6 sprint tasks marked completed in Asana with a comment citing file paths and line numbers (mirror the close convention used 2026-06-04).
All new tests pass: cd app && npm test.

Run cd app && npm run dev and smoke-test: trash icon gone from rows, cancel/postpone requires note, /today renders the new four-section layout, /daily redirects to /today, Inbound Pipeline verification doc complete, MVP QA pass checklist complete.
Update CLAUDE.md: route /daily -> /today (note the redirect), 'Today' page section structure documented under Page Map, mention TaskActionNoteModal mandatory-note behavior.
Update audit_map.md in the sprint-planner skill: TodayView.helpers.ts replaces DailyView.helpers.ts, ColumnPicker stays.
Update memory project_sanders_intelligence.md if the route map or task lifecycle changed materially (mandatory notes are a lifecycle change).

After this phase, the next planning pass should re-evaluate: 1215210678717164 (claim flow - likely absorbed by task #5 if user picks the recommended scope), the deferred 1215328973153040 (News feed), 1214539638231257 (Mobile responsiveness).
